title: 怪屋
summary:
Roger realizes that his company is facing inevitable bankruptcy, which brings him a sense of relief as he desires to start a new life with Clementine Leonidis. Clementine reveals her plan to move to Barbados to live a simple, carefree life using a small inheritance from a deceased relative. She expresses indifference toward money, noting she was happiest when she was poor with her late husband, Richard. When the narrator suggests using money for research, Clementine dismisses the idea, claiming donations are often wasted. Regarding the ongoing investigation, Clementine avoids speculation but identifies Brenda Leontidis and Lawrence Brown as the recognized suspects.

Edith de Havilland approaches the narrator to discuss the family dynamics. She describes Philip as a cold, polite man who is secretly jealous of Roger and appears secretly pleased by Roger's failures, noting that Philip failed to help Roger during his crisis. Edith characterizes Clementine as an incomprehensible person who prefers an uncomfortable lifestyle. While Edith expresses care for Macey's children—Sophia, Eustace, and Josephine—she warns of the family's tradition of "blind worship."

Later, the narrator and Sophia observe Brenda Leontidis and Lawrence Brown approaching the house through the garden. Sophia expresses concern for Eustace, noting his moody and unpredictable behavior following a bout with polio, which she attributes to his parents' lack of involvement. Sophia concludes that her parents are strange people; her father is preoccupied with unverified history, while her mother creates stage personas and seeks to stir up drama.
